title: Lea T fronts Redken as first openly transgender face of a global cosmetics brand
description: Brazilian model Lea T signed as the face of the American hair-care brand Redken, the first time an openly transgender model represented a cosmetics brand worldwide.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: CDC campaign features its first openly transgender spokesperson
description: Jennifer Barge served as spokesperson for a national US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention campaign, the agency's first openly transgender campaign face.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chris Mosier enters National Gay and Lesbian Sports Hall of Fame
description: Triathlete Chris Mosier was inducted into the US National Gay and Lesbian Sports Hall of Fame, its first openly transgender man.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kinnon MacKinnon wins Gay Games powerlifting gold as openly transgender man
description: At the 2014 Gay Games in Cleveland, Kinnon MacKinnon took a gold medal in powerlifting, the first openly transgender man to do so at the Games.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: BBC2 commissions Boy Meets Girl, Britain's first transgender sitcom
description: BBC2 ordered Boy Meets Girl, the first British sitcom centered on a transgender character, following the relationship between a 26-year-old man and a 40-year-old transgender woman.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: ICEIS Rain performs at Aboriginal Peoples Choice Music Awards as first two-spirit artist
description: Singer ICEIS Rain became the first openly two-spirit performer to appear on stage at Canada's Aboriginal Peoples Choice Music Awards.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Padmini Prakash becomes India's first openly transgender news anchor
description: Padmini Prakash began presenting television news in India, the country's first openly transgender news anchor.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Denmark drops psychiatric diagnosis requirement for legal gender recognition
description: Denmark removed the Gender Identity Disorder diagnosis as a precondition for changing legal gender, the first European country to base gender recognition on self-determination rather than a psychiatric label.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Malta writes gender identity into its constitution as a protected category
description: Malta added gender identity to the protected categories in its constitution, the first European state to give it constitutional protection.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly transgender woman marries in Malta
description: Malta saw its first wedding of an openly transgender woman, a personal milestone that followed the country's advances in gender recognition.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Transgender Bangladeshis hold the country's first pride march
description: At least 1,000 openly transgender people marched in Bangladesh's first pride parade, one year after the government legally recognized hijras as a third gender.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sochi Winter Olympics draw worldwide protests over Russia's anti-LGBT laws
description: The 2014 Winter Olympics in Sochi prompted protests around the world against host country Russia's crackdown on LGBT rights. Gus Kenworthy, later openly gay, won silver in men's freestyle skiing.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Conner Mertens becomes first active college football player to come out
description: Willamette University kicker Conner Mertens came out as bisexual, the first active US college football player to come out as LGBT.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Derrick Gordon comes out as first openly gay Division I men's basketball player
description: University of Massachusetts guard Derrick Gordon came out, making him the first openly gay player in NCAA Division I men's basketball.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Michael Sam becomes first openly gay player drafted into the NFL
description: The St. Louis Rams selected Michael Sam in the NFL draft, making him the first openly gay player drafted into the National Football League.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Arizona approves its first transgender student-athlete in a winter sport
description: The Arizona Interscholastic Association's executive board cleared the state's first transgender student-athlete to compete in a winter sport.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Edward Sarafin becomes first active Division I football player to come out as gay
description: Arizona State backup offensive lineman Edward Sarafin came out as gay while on the roster, the first active NCAA Division I football player to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Strongman Rob Kearney comes out as gay
description: Professional strongman Rob Kearney came out, becoming the first openly gay man actively competing at international professional strongman events.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First bodybuilding competition for transgender men held
description: The FTM Fitness Conference staged the FTM Fitness World Bodybuilding Competition, the first bodybuilding contest exclusively for transgender men.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Derrick Gordon plays as first openly gay athlete in a Division I basketball game
description: Derrick Gordon took the court for UMass after coming out, the first openly gay athlete to play in an NCAA Division I men's basketball game.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Dale Scott comes out as first openly gay Major League Baseball umpire
description: Veteran umpire Dale Scott came out as gay, making him the first openly gay umpire in Major League Baseball.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Robbie Rogers wins MLS Cup as first openly gay champion in major US team sports
description: When the LA Galaxy took the Major League Soccer Cup, Robbie Rogers became the first openly gay male athlete to win a major professional team-sports title in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Disney Channel airs its first same-sex couple on Good Luck Charlie
description: The series Good Luck Charlie introduced characters Susan and Cheryl, the first same-sex couple shown on a television network aimed at children.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Finland issues postage stamps honoring Tom of Finland
description: Finland's postal service released stamps celebrating Touko Laaksonen, the homoerotic artist known as Tom of Finland, bringing his work onto official state stamps.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First Jewish and Moroccan boats join Amsterdam Pride Canal Parade
description: The Amsterdam Pride Canal Parade included its first Jewish boat, with Reform rabbi Marianne van Praag of The Hague aboard, alongside the parade's first Moroccan boat.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Cosmopolitan prints sex advice for lesbians for the first time
description: The women's magazine Cosmopolitan published sex advice aimed at lesbian readers, a first in the publication's history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Pascal Tessier becomes first known openly gay Eagle Scout
description: Pascal Tessier, a 17-year-old from Chevy Chase, Maryland, attained the rank of Eagle Scout, the first known openly gay Boy Scout to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: United Church of Christ sponsors the Gay Games as first religious denomination
description: The United Church of Christ backed Gay Games 9 as a silver-level sponsor, the first religious denomination to act as a major sponsor of the Games.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Maria Walsh becomes first openly gay Rose of Tralee
description: After winning Ireland's Rose of Tralee title, Maria Walsh came out as gay, the first openly gay holder of the title.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Pakistan's first anti-homophobia children's book appears in print
description: My Chacha Is Gay, written and illustrated by the Canada-based artist Eiynah, was released in print as Pakistan's first children's book challenging homophobia, after first circulating online.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tel Aviv unveils memorial to LGBT victims of Nazi persecution
description: A memorial honoring LGBT people persecuted by the Nazi regime was unveiled in Tel Aviv, Israel's first monument dedicated to non-Jewish victims of the Holocaust.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mikie Goldstein ordained as first openly gay Conservative Jewish rabbi
description: Mikie Goldstein received ordination in Judaism's Conservative movement, its first openly gay man to be ordained as a rabbi.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mikie Goldstein installed as Israel's first openly gay Conservative pulpit rabbi
description: Congregation Adat Shalom-Emanuel in Rehovot installed Mikie Goldstein as its spiritual leader, the Israeli Conservative movement's first openly gay congregational rabbi.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Family Circle magazine features a same-sex couple for the first time
description: The November 2014 issue of Family Circle included a same-sex couple, a first for the long-running American family magazine.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nehirim holds first retreat for LGBT Jewish clergy
description: The Jewish organization Nehirim convened its first retreat for LGBT rabbis, rabbinic pastors, cantors, and students of those professions.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Los Tigres del Norte release the first norteño song about lesbian love
description: The album Realidades by Los Tigres del Norte included "Era Diferente", a song about a lesbian teenager in love with her best friend — described by bandleader Jorge Hernandez as the first gay love song by a norteño group.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: UN Human Rights Council passes second resolution on sexual orientation and gender identity
description: The UN Human Rights Council adopted its second resolution addressing sexual orientation and gender identity by a 25-14 vote, the first LGBT-rights resolution backed by a majority of the Council's members.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: South Africa seats Africa's first openly gay cabinet minister and MP
description: Lynne Brown was appointed to South Africa's cabinet, the first openly gay minister in any African government, while Zakhele Mbhele entered parliament as the first openly gay black MP in any African nation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Petra De Sutter enters Belgian Senate as first openly transgender parliamentarian
description: Petra De Sutter took a seat in Belgium's Senate, the first openly transgender person to serve in the country's Parliament.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Peru gains its first openly transgender official and first openly gay congressman
description: Luisa Revilla Urcia won a council seat in La Esperanza, Trujillo province, the first openly transgender person elected to public office in Peru. Congressman Carlos Bruce later came out, the first openly gay member of Peru's Congress.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Edgars Rinkēvičs comes out as Latvia's first openly gay lawmaker
description: Latvian politician Edgars Rinkēvičs announced he is gay, the first lawmaker in Latvia to do so and the most prominent openly gay politician in any former Soviet Bloc state at the time.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Robert Biedroń elected as Poland's first openly gay mayor
description: Voters in Słupsk elected Robert Biedroń mayor, the first openly gay person to lead a Polish city.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Matthew Muir sworn in as first openly gay judge on New Zealand's High Court
description: Matthew Muir joined the bench of New Zealand's High Court, the first openly gay judge to sit on it.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Victoria appoints Australia's first minister dedicated to LGBTI equality
description: The incoming Labor government of Victoria, Australia named Martin Foley Minister for Equality, the first Australian government post dedicated to gay, lesbian, bisexual, transgender and intersex issues.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Andrew Barr becomes Australia's first openly gay head of a state or territory government
description: Andrew Barr was sworn in as chief minister of the Australian Capital Territory, the first openly gay leader of a state or territory government in Australia.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Maite Oronoz Rodriguez confirmed to Puerto Rico's Supreme Court
description: Maite Oronoz Rodriguez was nominated and then confirmed to Puerto Rico's Supreme Court, the first openly gay person to reach the territory's highest bench.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Darrin P. Gayles confirmed as first openly gay African-American male federal judge
description: The US Senate confirmed Darrin P. Gayles to the federal bench, the first openly gay African-American man to become a US federal judge.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Judith Ellen Levy confirmed as Michigan's first openly lesbian federal judge
description: The US Senate confirmed Judith Ellen Levy to the federal bench, making her the first openly lesbian federal judge in Michigan.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Toni Atkins elected first openly lesbian speaker of the California Assembly
description: Toni Atkins became the first openly lesbian speaker of the California State Assembly, and in that role served as the state's acting governor for a day.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First US national campaign ad to feature a same-sex couple airs
description: Senate candidate Monica Wehby ran a campaign advertisement showing couple Ben West and Paul Rummell, the first ad for American national office to feature a same-sex couple.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
